tick_source: alps_loop_20260508T013943Z (reported beads-leakage-count=36 routed to flywheel:1 at 2026-05-08T01:39Z)

peer_orch_mistake_doctrine_wire_beads.description: These are flywheel doctrine wire-or-explain beads. They were created in alpsinsurance/.beads/issues.jsonl by a peer orch (alpsinsurance) running a doctrine-propagation pass that misrouted writes to its own bead store instead of the flywheel repo. All 55 have source_repo=/Users/josh/Developer/alpsinsurance (so they were minted via br create from alps cwd) but their content scope is flywheel doctrine wiring, not alps app work.

sweep_branches.action: br --no-db move <id> --from alpsinsurance --to flywheel (or jq-based JSONL surgery + br sync per repo) — preserves IDs and citation history
sweep_branches.risk: med (cross-repo move requires both repos' integrity_check to pass before+after; canonical move tooling does not exist yet — may need beads_rust feature request to Jeff)

sweep_branches.action: br close <id> --reason 'misrouted to alps; recreated in flywheel as flywheel-wire-<slug>-<new-hash>' for each; then br create from flywheel cwd with the same title/description
sweep_branches.risk: low (idempotent; preserves doctrinal intent; loses the original ID hash but reference history is in the new bead's description)

sweep_branches.action: br close <id> --reason 'misrouted; doctrine already wired elsewhere; closing as duplicate' for each — appropriate when the wire-rule is already shipped via INCIDENTS.md or AGENTS.md
sweep_branches.risk: low (drops bead but preserves doctrinal effect via INCIDENTS evidence)

cross_project_escalation_pattern.description: Beads created when ALPS workers needed to escalate blockers to /flywheel:plan for cross-orch resolution. The escalation pattern is legitimate, but the resulting beads currently live in the escalating-repo's store (alps), not the receiving-orch's store (flywheel). Either location is defensible; the policy choice affects where /flywheel:plan triage looks for them.

sweep_branches.action: rename id prefix from flywheel-escalate-* to alps-escalate-* (or josh-escalate-*) so the bead's host repo and id prefix agree — preserves locality but breaks the 'flywheel-' prefix-as-cross-orch-handoff signal
move all 8 from alps store to flywheel store via the same move-or-recreate pattern as the wire beads
sweep_branches.rationale: matches the cross-orch handoff semantics: an escalation TARGETED at /flywheel:plan should be visible in the flywheel repo's br ready output by default

test_sandbox_tmp_traces.description: Two beads with id prefix tmp- and source_repo=.tmpcZfXXP (a relative tmpfs path). Created within 100ms of each other on 2026-05-06T17:19. These are clearly test-sandbox traces from a test fixture run that misrouted into the live alps bead store.

sweep_branches.action: br --no-db delete tmp-8aw && br --no-db delete tmp-8iq (or JSONL filter + br sync) — these are clearly test detritus with a tmpfs source_repo that no longer exists

recommended_sweep_policy_aggregate.policy_summary: Three-tier sweep: (1) delete 2 tmp-* test traces; (2) close 55 flywheel-wire-* beads in alps with reason='misrouted; doctrine wired elsewhere' since most have already-shipped wire-rule beads in flywheel; (3) move 8 flywheel-escalate-* beads from alps to flywheel store (per cross-orch handoff semantics) OR rename prefix to alps-escalate-* if move tooling is unavailable.

phase_4_followups.title_hint: [alps-substrate-sweep] delete 2 tmp-* test traces from alps bead store
[alps-substrate-sweep] close 55 flywheel-wire-* misrouted doctrine beads in alps
phase_4_followups.rollback: br --no-db reopen <id> for each closed bead
branch-specific (depends on move vs rename branch)

structural_findings.primary_root_cause_observation: All 63 flywheel-prefixed beads in alps share source_repo=/Users/josh/Developer/alpsinsurance, meaning br create was invoked from alps cwd while the title/scope was flywheel-doctrine work. This is a peer-orch cwd-mistake class trauma; the prefix is not the root cause — the root cause is workers using br create from the wrong cwd during cross-orch doctrine propagation passes.
structural_findings.secondary_observation: All 65 leaks are status=open (none closed via the normal br close path); none have mirrors in the flywheel repo. This is consistent with workers creating but never updating these beads.
structural_findings.growth_signal: The bead reported count=36 from 2026-05-08T01:39Z; the live probe (this dispatch) found 65. ~29 new leaks accumulated in approximately 36 hours (80.5% growth). The bleed is active, not historical.
structural_findings.isolation_doctrine_alignment: Aligns with project_bead_isolation_plan memory rule (4-phase plan to fix 8 cross-project leakage FMs) and feedback_basename_keying_collision_class (basename keying causes cross-project bleed). The wire-rule beads are an instance of FM4 (doctrine propagation writes to wrong store).

negative_invariants_checked.no_writes_to_alps_jsonl_by_this_dispatch: PASS — read-only probe; no br create/close/delete invoked
negative_invariants_checked.no_writes_to_flywheel_jsonl_by_this_disp: PASS — no follow-up beads filed; recommended Phase 4 follow-ups left as policy artifacts in this report

rollback.side_effects: none beyond removing this report; alps and flywheel bead stores untouched

secret_scan_method: no secret-class fields in this report; only bead IDs, titles, file paths, source_repo strings, and counts are recorded
